headline: Pfizer CEO Backs Trump's CDC Pick After Vaccine Policy Upheaval
summary: Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) chief executive Albert Bourla has signaled a possible shift in tone around US vaccine policy, praising President Donald Trump's latest nominee to lead the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention after a turbulent year for the agency.  In a Bloomberg TV interview Tuesday, Bourla called Erica Schwartz a very credible scientist, pointing to what could be a more conventional choice for the country's top public health role.  Schwartz, who holds both medical and law degrees and previously served as deputy surgeon general during the first Trump administration, is now being viewed as a figure who could help stabilize an agency that has become increasingly important for vaccine makers.

headline: CellCentric Announces Oversubscribed $220 Million Series D Financing to Advance Pivotal Trials of Inobrodib in Multiple Myeloma
summary: CAMBRIDGE, England & BOSTON, May 06, 2026--CellCentric, a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing inobrodib as a first-in-class, oral p300/CBP inhibitor for the treatment of multiple myeloma, today announced the completion of an oversubscribed $220 million Series D financing. The financing was led by specialist investor Venrock Healthcare Capital Partners, with participation from a strong syndicate of new and existing investors.
